req.title: Export employee directory — variant 3
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three orthogonal concerns: (1) happy-path CSV export with PII redaction for opted-out employees, (2) access-control enforcement (403 for non-hr-admin), (3) rate-limit enforcement (429), (4) webhook emission on export, and (5) boundary behaviour at the rate-limit threshold. EP is applied to role partitions and opt-out partitions; NEG covers forbidden role and rate-limit breach; BVA covers the request count at the rate-limit edge; DT covers the opt-out/non-opt-out combination; STC covers the state change implied by the webhook emission side-effect. Five tests are generated to ensure adequate coverage of all stated outcomes and error pathways.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out_employees
preconditions: Caller has role hr-admin (valid JWT supplied via <hr_admin_token> fixture)
At least one employee has opted out of public directory (seed via <seed_opted_out_employee> fixture)
At least one employee has NOT opted out (seed via <seed_active_employee> fixture)
expected: Response body is valid CSV with a header row
Opted-out employee row has PII fields (email, phone, address) replaced with REDACTED
Non-opted-out employee row contains real PII values
tests.executable:
def test_hr_admin_export_csv_pii_redacted_for_opted_out_employees(
    hr_admin_token, seed_opted_out_employee, seed_active_employee, api_client
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
        stream=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "text/csv" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")

    rows = <parse_csv_response>(resp.text)
    assert len(rows) >= 2, "Expected header + at least two data rows"

    opted_out_row = <find_row_by_id>(rows, seed_opted_out_employee["id"])
    active_row = <find_row_by_id>(rows, seed_active_employee["id"])

    for pii_field in ("email", "phone", "address"):
        assert opted_out_row[pii_field] == "REDACTED"
        assert active_row[pii_field] != "REDACTED"
        assert active_row[pii_field] == seed_active_employee[pii_field]

tests.name: test_non_hr_admin_export_csv_returns_403
preconditions: Caller has role employee (not hr-admin) — token supplied via <employee_token> fixture
expected: Response body contains error code or message indicating forbidden access
tests.executable:
def test_non_hr_admin_export_csv_returns_403(employee_token, api_client):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body

tests.name: test_export_csv_rate_limit_boundary_returns_429_on_exceeding_limit
preconditions: Caller has role hr-admin (valid JWT supplied via <hr_admin_token> fixture)
Rate-limit quota is known via <RATE_LIMIT_MAX> constant (e.g. 10 requests per minute)
Rate-limit counter has been reset for this token via <reset_rate_limit> fixture
actions: Send exactly RATE_LIMIT_MAX requests to GET /api/employees/export.csv — all must succeed
Send one additional (RATE_LIMIT_MAX + 1) request immediately after
expected: First RATE_LIMIT_MAX requests each return HTTP 200
trace: BVA applied at N and N+1 boundary of rate-limit window

tests.name: test_export_csv_emits_webhook_event_on_success
preconditions: Caller has role hr-admin (valid JWT supplied via <hr_admin_token> fixture)
Webhook receiver is configured and listening via <webhook_receiver> fixture
Webhook receiver inbox is empty before the test (<webhook_receiver>.clear())
actions: Wait up to <WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS> for webhook receiver to capture an event
expected: Webhook receiver captures exactly one event within the timeout
Captured event payload contains actor field equal to the hr-admin user id
trace: R-WEB-084: additional variant semantics honoured — webhook event emitted for external consumers
tests.executable:
def test_export_csv_emits_webhook_event_on_success(
    hr_admin_token, webhook_receiver, api_client, WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS
):
    webhook_receiver.clear()
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    event = <wait_for_webhook_event>(
        webhook_receiver,
        timeout=WEBHOOK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS,
        event_type="employee_directory_exported",
    )
    assert event is not None, "No webhook event received within timeout"
    assert event["event_type"] == "employee_directory_exported"
    admin_id = <decode_token_subject>(hr_admin_token)
    assert event["payload"]["actor"] == admin_id

tests.name: test_export_csv_decision_table_all_employees_opted_out_all_pii_redacted
preconditions: Caller has role hr-admin (valid JWT supplied via <hr_admin_token> fixture)
ALL employees in the directory have opted out of public directory (seed via <seed_all_opted_out_employees> fixture)
expected: Every data row (excluding header) has REDACTED in all PII columns (email, phone, address)
Non-PII columns (e.g. employee_id, department) retain real values
trace: R-WEB-084: PII redacted per opt-out — DT cell: all employees opted-out => all PII redacted
